intro: The biggest real split between Apify and Backstage is lock-in posture: Apify carries medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable), while Backstage carries high lock-in (oss spotify platform). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Apify is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. Backstage is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. On the scoring side, Apify rates 5/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; Backstage rates 4/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Apify you accept that simple api-to-api integrations, while Backstage comes with the reality that simple api-to-api integrations. Neither side is free. Apify's headline strength in the data is that data warehouse syncing. Backstage's headline strength is that data warehouse syncing. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Apify is for data warehouse syncing; Backstage is for data warehouse syncing; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Apify if you are data warehouse syncing and can accept medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable).
verdict_b: Choose Backstage if you are data warehouse syncing and need high lock-in (oss spotify platform).

faq.a: Both list a freemium pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Apify to Backstage?
faq.a: Apify has medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable); Backstage has high lock-in (oss spotify platform). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Apify; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Apify scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Backstage. That said, DX is subjective; the Backstage score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Backstage a good alternative to Apify?
faq.a: Backstage is built for data warehouse syncing and explicitly not for simple, small projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Apify for data warehouse syncing, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
